NKJV.1: I saw still another mighty angel coming down from heaven, clothed with a cloud. And a rainbow was on his head, his face was like the sun, and his feet like pillars of fire.
NKJV.2: He had a little book open in his hand. And he set his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land,
NKJV.3: and cried with a loud voice, as when a lion roars. When he cried out, seven thunders uttered their voices.
NKJV.4: Now when the seven thunders uttered their voices, I was about to write; but I heard a voice from heaven saying to me, “Seal up the things which the seven thunders uttered, and do not write them.”
NKJV.5: The angel whom I saw standing on the sea and on the land raised up his hand to heaven
NKJV.6: and swore by Him who lives forever and ever, who created heaven and the things that are in it, the earth and the things that are in it, and the sea and the things that are in it, that there should be delay no longer,
NKJV.7: but in the days of the sounding of the seventh angel, when he is about to sound, the mystery of God would be finished, as He declared to His servants the prophets.
NKJV.8: Then the voice which I heard from heaven spoke to me again and said, “Go, take the little book which is open in the hand of the angel who stands on the sea and on the earth.”
NKJV.9: So I went to the angel and said to him, “Give me the little book.” And he said to me, “Take and eat it; and it will make your stomach bitter, but it will be as sweet as honey in your mouth.”
NKJV.10: Then I took the little book out of the angel’s hand and ate it, and it was as sweet as honey in my mouth. But when I had eaten it, my stomach became bitter.
NKJV.11: And he said to me, “You must prophesy again about many peoples, nations, tongues, and kings.”

ESV.1: Then I saw another mighty angel coming down from heaven, wrapped in a cloud, with a rainbow over his head, and his face was like the sun, and his legs like pillars of fire.
ESV.2: He had a little scroll open in his hand. And he set his right foot on the sea, and his left foot on the land,
ESV.3: and called out with a loud voice, like a lion roaring. When he called out, the seven thunders sounded.
ESV.4: And when the seven thunders had sounded, I was about to write, but I heard a voice from heaven saying, “Seal up what the seven thunders have said, and do not write it down.”
ESV.5: And the angel whom I saw standing on the sea and on the land raised his right hand to heaven
ESV.6: and swore by him who lives forever and ever, who created heaven and what is in it, the earth and what is in it, and the sea and what is in it, that there would be no more delay,
ESV.7: but that in the days of the trumpet call to be sounded by the seventh angel, the mystery of God would be fulfilled, just as he announced to his servants the prophets.
ESV.8: Then the voice that I had heard from heaven spoke to me again, saying, “Go, take the scroll that is open in the hand of the angel who is standing on the sea and on the land.”
ESV.9: So I went to the angel and told him to give me the little scroll. And he said to me, “Take and eat it; it will make your stomach bitter, but in your mouth it will be sweet as honey.”
ESV.10: And I took the little scroll from the hand of the angel and ate it. It was sweet as honey in my mouth, but when I had eaten it my stomach was made bitter.
ESV.11: And I was told, “You must again prophesy about many peoples and nations and languages and kings.”

NASB.1: I saw another strong angel coming down out of heaven, clothed with a cloud; and the rainbow was upon his head, and his face was like the sun, and his feet like pillars of fire;
NASB.2: and he had in his hand a little book which was open. He placed his right foot on the sea and his left on the land;
NASB.3: and he cried out with a loud voice, as when a lion roars; and when he had cried out, the seven peals of thunder uttered their voices.
NASB.4: When the seven peals of thunder had spoken, I was about to write; and I heard a voice from heaven saying, 'Seal up the things which the seven peals of thunder have spoken and do not write them.'
NASB.5: Then the angel whom I saw standing on the sea and on the land lifted up his right hand to heaven,
NASB.6: and swore by Him who lives forever and ever, WHO CREATED HEAVEN AND THE THINGS IN IT, AND THE EARTH AND THE THINGS IN IT, AND THE SEA AND THE THINGS IN IT, that there will be delay no longer,
NASB.7: but in the days of the voice of the seventh angel, when he is about to sound, then the mystery of God is finished, as He preached to His servants the prophets.
NASB.8: Then the voice which I heard from heaven, [I heard] again speaking with me, and saying, 'Go, take the book which is open in the hand of the angel who stands on the sea and on the land.'
NASB.9: So I went to the angel, telling him to give me the little book. And he said to me, 'Take it and eat it; it will make your stomach bitter, but in your mouth it will be sweet as honey.'
NASB.10: I took the little book out of the angel's hand and ate it, and in my mouth it was sweet as honey; and when I had eaten it, my stomach was made bitter.
NASB.11: And they said to me, 'You must prophesy again concerning many peoples and nations and tongues and kings.'

NLT.1: Then I saw another mighty angel coming down from heaven, surrounded by a cloud, with a rainbow over his head. His face shone like the sun, and his feet were like pillars of fire.
NLT.2: And in his hand was a small scroll that had been opened. He stood with his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
NLT.3: And he gave a great shout like the roar of a lion. And when he shouted, the seven thunders answered.
NLT.4: When the seven thunders spoke, I was about to write. But I heard a voice from heaven saying, “Keep secret what the seven thunders said, and do not write it down.”
NLT.5: Then the angel I saw standing on the sea and on the land raised his right hand toward heaven.
NLT.6: He swore an oath in the name of the one who lives forever and ever, who created the heavens and everything in them, the earth and everything in it, and the sea and everything in it. He said, “There will be no more delay.
NLT.7: When the seventh angel blows his trumpet, God’s mysterious plan will be fulfilled. It will happen just as he announced it to his servants the prophets.”
NLT.8: Then the voice from heaven spoke to me again: “Go and take the open scroll from the hand of the angel who is standing on the sea and on the land.”
NLT.9: So I went to the angel and told him to give me the small scroll. “Yes, take it and eat it,” he said. “It will be sweet as honey in your mouth, but it will turn sour in your stomach!”
NLT.10: So I took the small scroll from the hand of the angel, and I ate it! It was sweet in my mouth, but when I swallowed it, it turned sour in my stomach.
NLT.11: Then I was told, “You must prophesy again about many peoples, nations, languages, and kings.”

WEB.1: I saw a mighty angel coming down out of the sky, clothed with a cloud. A rainbow was on his head. His face was like the sun, and his feet like pillars of fire.
WEB.2: He had in his hand a little open book. He set his right foot on the sea, and his left on the land.
WEB.3: He cried with a loud voice, as a lion roars. When he cried, the seven thunders uttered their voices.
WEB.4: When the seven thunders sounded, I was about to write; but I heard a voice from the sky saying, “Seal up the things which the seven thunders said, and don’t write them.”
WEB.5: The angel whom I saw standing on the sea and on the land lifted up his right hand to the sky,
WEB.6: and swore by him who lives forever and ever, who created heaven and the things that are in it, the earth and the things that are in it, and the sea and the things that are in it, that there will no longer be delay,
WEB.7: but in the days of the voice of the seventh angel, when he is about to sound, then the mystery of God is finished, as he declared to his servants, the prophets.
WEB.8: The voice which I heard from heaven, again speaking with me, said, “Go, take the book which is open in the hand of the angel who stands on the sea and on the land.”
WEB.9: I went to the angel, telling him to give me the little book. He said to me, “Take it, and eat it up. It will make your stomach bitter, but in your mouth it will be as sweet as honey.”
WEB.10: I took the little book out of the angel’s hand, and ate it up. It was as sweet as honey in my mouth. When I had eaten it, my stomach was made bitter.
WEB.11: They told me, “You must prophesy again over many peoples, nations, languages, and kings.”

CSB17.1: Then I saw another mighty angel <sup>ⓚ</sup> coming down from heaven, wrapped in a cloud, with a rainbow <sup>ⓛ</sup> over his head. <sup>[1]</sup> His face was like the sun, his legs <sup>[2]</sup> were like pillars of fire, <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.2: and he held a little scroll opened in his hand. He put his right foot on the sea, his left on the land,
CSB17.3: and he called out with a loud voice like a roaring lion. When he cried out, the seven thunders raised their voices.
CSB17.4: And when the seven thunders spoke, I was about to write, but I heard a voice from heaven, saying, “Seal up what the seven thunders said, <sup>ⓝ</sup> and do not write it down!”
CSB17.5: Then the angel that I had seen standing on the sea and on the land raised his right hand to heaven.
CSB17.6: He swore by the one who lives forever and ever, <sup>ⓐ</sup> who created heaven and what is in it, the earth and what is in it, and the sea and what is in it: “There will no longer be a delay,
CSB17.7: but in the days when the seventh angel <sup>ⓑ</sup> will blow his trumpet, then the mystery of God <sup>ⓒ</sup> will be completed, as he announced to his servants the prophets.” <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.8: Then the voice that I heard from heaven spoke to me again and said, “Go, take the scroll that lies open in the hand of the angel who is standing on the sea and on the land.”
CSB17.9: So I went to the angel and asked him to give me the little scroll. He said to me, “Take and eat it; it will be bitter in your stomach, but it will be as sweet as honey in your mouth.” <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.10: Then I took the little scroll from the angel’s hand and ate it. It was as sweet as honey in my mouth, but when I ate it, my stomach became bitter. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.11: And they said to me, “You must prophesy again about <sup>[3]</sup> many peoples, nations, languages, and kings.” <sup>ⓖ</sup>

AMP.1: Then I saw another mighty angel coming down from heaven, clothed in a cloud, with a rainbow (halo) over his head; and his face was like the sun, and his feet (legs) were like columns of fire;
AMP.2: and he had a little book (scroll) open in his hand. He set his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land;
AMP.3: and he shouted with a loud voice, like the roaring of a lion [compelling attention and inspiring awe]; and when he had shouted out, the seven peals of thunder spoke with their own voices [uttering their message in distinct words].
AMP.4: And when the seven peals of thunder had spoken, I was about to write; but I heard a voice from heaven saying, "Seal up the things which the seven peals of thunder have spoken and do not write them down."
AMP.5: Then the angel whom I had seen standing on the sea and the land raised his right hand [to swear an oath] to heaven,
AMP.6: and swore [an oath] by [the name of] Him who lives forever and ever, who created heaven and the things in it, and the earth and the things in it, and the sea and the things in it, that there will be delay no longer,
AMP.7: but when it is time for the trumpet call of the seventh angel, when he is about to sound, then the mystery of God [that is, His hidden purpose and plan] is finished, as He announced the gospel to His servants the prophets.
AMP.8: Then the voice which I heard from heaven, I heard again speaking to me, and saying, "Go, take the book (scroll) which is open in the hand of the angel who is standing on the sea and on the land."
AMP.9: So I went up to the angel and told him to give me the little book. And he said to me, "Take it and eat it; it will make your stomach bitter, but in your mouth it will be as sweet as honey."
AMP.10: So I took the little book from the angel's hand and ate it, and in my mouth it was as sweet as honey; but once I had swallowed it, my stomach was bitter.
AMP.11: Then they said to me, "You must prophesy again concerning many peoples and nations and languages and kings."

MSG.1: I saw another powerful Angel coming down out of Heaven wrapped in a cloud. There was a rainbow over his head, his face was sun-radiant, his legs pillars of fire.
MSG.2: He had a small book open in his hand. He placed his right foot on the sea and his left foot on land,
MSG.3: then called out thunderously, a lion roar. When he called out, the Seven Thunders called back.
MSG.4: When the Seven Thunders spoke, I started to write it all down, but a voice out of Heaven stopped me, saying, "Seal with silence the Seven Thunders; don't write a word."
MSG.5: Then the Angel I saw astride sea and land lifted his right hand to Heaven
MSG.6: and swore by the One Living Forever and Ever, who created Heaven and everything in it, earth and everything in it, sea and everything in it, that time was up—
MSG.7: that when the seventh Angel blew his trumpet, which he was about to do, the Mystery of God, all the plans he had revealed to his servants, the prophets, would be completed.
MSG.8: The voice out of Heaven spoke to me again: "Go, take the book held open in the hand of the Angel astride sea and earth."
MSG.9: I went up to the Angel and said, "Give me the little book." He said, "Take it, then eat it. It will taste sweet like honey, but turn sour in your stomach."
MSG.10: I took the little book from the Angel's hand and it was sweet honey in my mouth, but when I swallowed, my stomach curdled.
MSG.11: Then I was told, "You must go back and prophesy again over many peoples and nations and languages and kings."

NIV.1:
The Angel and the Little Scroll
Then I saw another mighty angel coming down from heaven. He was robed in a cloud, with a rainbow above his head; his face was like the sun, and his legs were like fiery pillars.
NIV.2: He was holding a little scroll, which lay open in his hand. He planted his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land,
NIV.3: and he gave a loud shout like the roar of a lion. When he shouted, the voices of the seven thunders spoke.
NIV.4: And when the seven thunders spoke, I was about to write; but I heard a voice from heaven say, “Seal up what the seven thunders have said and do not write it down.”
NIV.5: Then the angel I had seen standing on the sea and on the land raised his right hand to heaven.
NIV.6: And he swore by him who lives for ever and ever, who created the heavens and all that is in them, the earth and all that is in it, and the sea and all that is in it, and said, “There will be no more delay!
NIV.7: But in the days when the seventh angel is about to sound his trumpet, the mystery of God will be accomplished, just as he announced to his servants the prophets.”
NIV.8: Then the voice that I had heard from heaven spoke to me once more: “Go, take the scroll that lies open in the hand of the angel who is standing on the sea and on the land.”
NIV.9: So I went to the angel and asked him to give me the little scroll. He said to me, “Take it and eat it. It will turn your stomach sour, but in your mouth it will be as sweet as honey.”
NIV.10: I took the little scroll from the angel’s hand and ate it. It tasted as sweet as honey in my mouth, but when I had eaten it, my stomach turned sour.
NIV.11: Then I was told, “You must prophesy again about many peoples, nations, languages and kings.”

NRSVCE.1: And I saw another mighty angel coming down from heaven, wrapped in a cloud, with a rainbow over his head; his face was like the sun, and his legs like pillars of fire.
NRSVCE.2: He held a little scroll open in his hand. Setting his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land,
NRSVCE.3: he gave a great shout, like a lion roaring. And when he shouted, the seven thunders sounded.
NRSVCE.4: And when the seven thunders had sounded, I was about to write, but I heard a voice from heaven saying, “Seal up what the seven thunders have said, and do not write it down.”
NRSVCE.5: Then the angel whom I saw standing on the sea and the land raised his right hand to heaven
NRSVCE.6:
and swore by him who lives forever and ever,
who created heaven and what is in it, the earth and what is in it, and the sea and what is in it: “There will be no more delay,
NRSVCE.7: but in the days when the seventh angel is to blow his trumpet, the mystery of God will be fulfilled, as he announced to his servants the prophets.”
NRSVCE.8: Then the voice that I had heard from heaven spoke to me again, saying, “Go, take the scroll that is open in the hand of the angel who is standing on the sea and on the land.”
NRSVCE.9: So I went to the angel and told him to give me the little scroll; and he said to me, “Take it, and eat; it will be bitter to your stomach, but sweet as honey in your mouth.”
NRSVCE.10: So I took the little scroll from the hand of the angel and ate it; it was sweet as honey in my mouth, but when I had eaten it, my stomach was made bitter.
NRSVCE.11: Then they said to me, “You must prophesy again about many peoples and nations and languages and kings.”

NET.1: Then I saw another powerful angel descending from heaven, wrapped in a cloud, with a rainbow above his head; his face was like the sun and his legs were like pillars of fire.
NET.2: He held in his hand a little scroll that was open, and he put his right foot on the sea and his left on the land.
NET.3: Then he shouted in a loud voice like a lion roaring, and when he shouted, the seven thunders sounded their voices.
NET.4: When the seven thunders spoke, I was preparing to write, but just then I heard a voice from heaven say, “Seal up what the seven thunders spoke and do not write it down.”
NET.5: Then the angel I saw standing on the sea and on the land raised his right hand to heaven
NET.6: and swore by the one who lives forever and ever, who created heaven and what is in it, and the earth and what is in it, and the sea and what is in it, “There will be no more delay!
NET.7: But in the days when the seventh angel is about to blow his trumpet, the mystery of God is completed, just as he has proclaimed to his servants the prophets.”
NET.8: Then the voice I had heard from heaven began to speak to me again, “Go and take the open scroll in the hand of the angel who is standing on the sea and on the land.”
NET.9: So I went to the angel and asked him to give me the little scroll. He said to me, “Take the scroll and eat it. It will make your stomach bitter, but it will be as sweet as honey in your mouth.”
NET.10: So I took the little scroll from the angel’s hand and ate it, and it did taste as sweet as honey in my mouth, but when I had eaten it, my stomach became bitter.
NET.11: Then they told me: “You must prophesy again about many peoples, nations, languages, and kings.”
